Health.detail: A newly identified brain protein may play a major role in how the body ages; researchers discovered that declining levels of Menin in the hypothalamus triggered inflammation, memory problems, bone issues, and other age-related problems, but a simple supplement reversed this decline in mice.
Health.headline: Scientists Discover Hidden Driver of Aging — Simple Supplement Reversed Brain Decline

Health.detail: Scientists have cracked open the “black box” of feline cancer in a landmark study that genetically analyzed nearly 500 cat tumors from around the world, uncovering striking similarities between cat and human cancers, potentially accelerating the development of new therapies.
Health.headline: Scientists Say House Cats Could Help Unlock New Cancer Treatments for Humans

Health.detail: Researchers at Duke University have discovered a potential new way to stop chronic nerve pain at its source by reviving damaged nerves.
Health.headline: Scientists “Recharge” Damaged Nerves to Ease Chronic Pain

Health.detail: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can dramatically reduce the body's ability to absorb healthy flavanols, showing that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually gets.
Health.headline: Surprising Research Reveals Why You Shouldn't Add Bananas to Your Smoothies

Health.detail: A study published this month by researchers at the University of Ottawa has found that applying woodchips to some trails significantly reduced local tick populations.
Health.headline: Woodchips on trails reduce tick populations, Ottawa researchers find

Entertainment.headline: Supergirl trailer brings back David Corenswet's Superman, Krypto's tragic fate and Jason Momoa's Lobo debut
Entertainment.detail: A new trailer for Supergirl has offered fans their clearest look yet at the dynamic between Kara Zor-El and David Corenswet's Superman, while positioning Krypto the Superdog at the emotional centre of the film.

Entertainment.headline: Disney's new Star Wars film Mandalorian on track to gross $211 million in global opening
Entertainment.detail: Disney's new Star Wars film 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' is expected to gross $211 million in its global opening weekend.

Entertainment.headline: Kyle Busch Honored During Indy 500 Days After His Death at Age 41
Entertainment.detail: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch was honored at the Indy 500 following his death at age 41.

A video was released showing Britney Spears' DUI arrest on March 4, 2026, in Ventura County, California, where officers smelled alcohol and conducted a field sobriety test. Spears later pleaded guilty to reckless driving.
Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears argument, DUI arrest on video
Goodbye cutesy, hello techno: Girl groups embrace harder, faster, louder sounds as trends shift
Entertainment.detail: K-pop girl groups are shifting away from cutesy themes and embracing harder, faster techno sounds, aligning with short-form video promotion trends. Blackpink is seen as one of the groups that helped bring the genre back into the mainstream global spotlight recently.

Politics.headline: Peacekeeping in peril amid plummeting troop numbers and geopolitical deadlock: New SIPRI report
Politics.detail: Geopolitical tensions, political pressure, and funding crises are jeopardizing multilateral peacekeeping, with personnel numbers at their lowest in 25 years, according to a new SIPRI analysis. The number of personnel in peace operations at the end of 2025 fell to its lowest point in at least 25 years.

Premiers from western provinces will meet in Kananaskis for two days starting Monday amid pipeline tensions between B.C. Premier David Eby and Alberta Premier Danielle Smith. Both premiers have acknowledged potential awkwardness at the meeting.
Politics.headline: Western premiers to meet in Alberta amid pipeline tensions between Smith and Eby

Politics.detail: A suspect is dead after opening fire near a White House security checkpoint. The Secret Service is investigating the incident.
Politics.headline: Suspect dead after opening fire near White House security checkpoint, Secret Service says

Politics.detail: Teal independents in Australia are reportedly in discussions to form a new political party, driven by voter disillusionment. Teal MPs Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall are reportedly leading the push, with planning underway for months.
Politics.headline: Teals in talks to form new political party: Report

Politics.detail: The Security Council is expected to vote on a resolution renewing the authorization for member states to inspect vessels off the coast of Libya that are believed to be violating the arms embargo, with the current authorization expiring on May 25, 2026. The Council is also expected to receive a briefing on the International Criminal Court's activities related to the situation in Libya.
Politics.headline: Libya, May 2026 Monthly Forecast

Zimbabwe.headline: Bee project advances Zim's bid to export honey to EU
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has taken a decisive step toward accessing the European Union honey market with a bee project.
The Parliament is consolidating public submissions regarding the Amendment No.3 Bill.
Zimbabwe.headline: Parly consolidates public submissions on Amendment No.3 Bill

Zimbabwe.detail: CAPS United has had ten days to reflect and recover from the pain of conceding a last-gasp goal.
Zimbabwe.headline: CAPS BACK IN ACTION AFTER SIMBA HEARTBREAK
VFEX-listed WestProp posts robust Q1 growth
Zimbabwe.detail: Victoria Falls Stock Exchange-listed property developer WestProp Holdings delivered a strong performance for the quarter ended March.
African institutions of higher learning, research centers and innovators should continue to develop homegrown solutions.
Zimbabwe.headline: ‘African solutions to African problems’

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Bato hits rock bottom
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Supreme Court rejected Sen. Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa's plea to stop his arrest by the ICC, and the Justice Secretary ordered the hunt for him to begin, leaving him with diminishing political protection.

After more than two decades of activism, Indonesia's parliament passed legislation classifying domestic workers as workers, ensuring they are entitled to health insurance, days off, and pensions, and activists say this example shows that persistent lobbying of individual legislators and helping workers to share their stories is critical.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Indonesians are fighting for and winning their rights
Antisemitism an issue for both major parties. Voters must reject it.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: San Antonio voters will show us how deep antisemitism runs in our community and Democratic primary voters should reject Maureen Galndo's antisemitism because there should be no tolerance for intolerance.
This opinion piece likely discusses the felling of trees on Quirino Avenue, potentially criticizing the action and its environmental impact.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: The massacre of Quirino Avenue trees.

Opinion/Editorial.detail: The growing popularity of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) points to a deeper problem that many institutions today seem to lack transparency and a meaningful connection with the people and there is also a growing perception that some of them are giving up their independent voice and avoiding speaking truth to power.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — May 25, 2026

Finance & Economy.headline: UK retail sales volumes drop 1.3% in April, biggest fall since May 2025
Finance & Economy.detail: UK retail sales volumes dropped 1.3% month-on-month in April 2026, worse than expectations of a 0.6% fall, mainly due to a 10.2% slump in fuel sales as motorists made fewer journeys and delayed refuelling amid higher prices.

US stocks advanced, buoyed by signs of progress in Middle East peace talks and a strong corporate earnings season, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.4%, extending its winning streak to eight consecutive weeks.
Finance & Economy.headline: US Stocks Extend Winning Streak Amid Middle East Peace Talks and Strong Earnings
AI Impact on US Labor Market: Suppressing Hiring
Finance & Economy.detail: Economists say AI is reshaping the U.S. labor market by suppressing hiring even as overall job losses remain limited.
Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the new Federal Reserve chair, replacing Powell, and vowed to preserve the Fed’s independence over monetary policy.
Finance & Economy.headline: Kevin Warsh Sworn in as New Fed Chair
U.S. International Trade Deficit Increased in March 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit increased in March 2026 to $60.3 billion, as imports increased more than exports, according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau.

Sports.detail: Tropang 5G, playing without an import, pulled off a surprising victory against the Bolts, putting them closer to the championship series.
Sports.headline: Import-less Tropang 5G stun Bolts to move within two wins of title series

Sports.detail: Chris McCullough stated that a call from RHJ (Rondae Hollis-Jefferson) influenced his decision to return to the PBA (Philippine Basketball Association) and join TNT.
Sports.headline: Chris McCullough says call from RHJ made PBA return with TNT happen
PBA: Caracut, Rain or Shine start hot but fizzle out in Game 3 loss
Sports.detail: Despite a strong start by Caracut and Rain or Shine, they were unable to maintain their momentum and suffered a loss in Game 3.

Sports.headline: 2026 UFL Week 9 Results: Chandler Rogers' Two TD Game Lifts Kings Over Renegades
Sports.detail: Chandler Rogers had a standout performance with two touchdowns, helping the Kings secure a victory over the Renegades in Week 9 of the UFL.
Erling Haaland has won his third Golden Boot, putting him on par with Harry Kane and Alan Shearer.
Sports.headline: Erling Haaland Wins Third Golden Boot To Match Harry Kane, Alan Shearer

Lifestyle.headline: When the sea speaks: M'sian sailors share stories of life far from shore
Lifestyle.detail: A new anthology, “Stories of the Seas”, brings together tales by former officers and engineers of Malaysian International Shipping Corporation Bhd (MISC), who served during the 1970s and '80s, sharing firsthand accounts of storms, sacrifice, humour and brotherhood.
Hotels are trying to improve their rankings in ChatGPT's travel recommendations, as AI-powered travel planning becomes more popular.
Lifestyle.headline: Hotels Want to Rank in ChatGPT's Travel Plans

Lifestyle.detail: Photos showcase the work of dogs brightening hospital days.
Lifestyle.headline: Healing with a wag and a nuzzle, meet the dogs brightening hospital days, in photos

Lifestyle.detail: Sara is proud of her brother Arjun's IPL 2026 debut and expresses her support.
Lifestyle.headline: Sara Is "Proud" Of Brother Arjun's IPL 2026 Debut: "You Deserve The World"
The lifestyle changes that could add an extra five years to your life
Lifestyle.detail: Healthy living could prolong the lives of some by five years.

Education.headline: Bank commits RM1mil annually to schools
Education.detail: A bank in Malaysia has launched a new school programme, committing RM1 million annually to provide sustained support to 40 schools nationwide, focusing on essential learning tools, enhanced school environments, and exposure to STEM and digital skills.

Education.source: Lakeshore School - Calgary Board of Education
Education.headline: Update to Parents - Week of May 25, 2026
Education.detail: Lakeshore School welcomes incoming Grade 6 students, as well as new Grade 7-9 students, and their families on Thursday night for their Open House, and any parents interested in learning the basics of Pickleball are welcome to join the intro event on Tuesday night.

Education.headline: Statement On Death Of Peter Buckskin PSM
Education.detail: Peter Buckskin, inaugural Chair of the National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Education Corporation (NATSIEC), has passed away; he advised the government across the education sector and championed culturally grounded teacher education and Indigenous research excellence.

Education.headline: Syrian educators welcome salary increase decree as step toward better education quality
Education.detail: Educators and academics in Syria said that the salary increase for workers in the education and higher education sectors represents an important step toward improving teachers' and faculty members' living conditions, strengthening job stability, and raising the quality of education.

Education.headline: Harvard faculty vote to limit A grades for undergraduates
Education.detail: In a closely watched vote, the faculty took strong action to combat grade inflation.

World.detail: President Trump stated that the U.S. will not be rushed into a peace deal with Iran, even as details of a potential agreement emerge, aiming to de-escalate the conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. However, disagreements persist over Iran's nuclear ambitions and sanctions.
World.headline: Trump says not to rush as U.S. nears potential Iran deal

World.detail: Russia launched a large-scale attack on Kyiv, using hypersonic missiles and drones, causing damage to residential buildings and schools and resulting in casualties. Ukrainian officials reported that the overnight barrage resulted in multiple deaths and injuries.
World.headline: Russia hits Kyiv with hypersonic missile in 'deranged' attack
Hajj 2026: Millions gather in Saudi for pilgrimage despite Middle East war
World.detail: Millions of pilgrims have gathered in Saudi Arabia for Hajj, even as the Middle East grapples with ongoing conflict. The main rituals are scheduled to begin on Monday, May 25, preceding Eid al-Adha.

World.headline: Peacekeeping in peril amid plummeting troop numbers and geopolitical deadlock: New SIPRI report
World.detail: A new report by the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) reveals that geopolitical tensions and funding crises are endangering multilateral peacekeeping, with personnel numbers at their lowest in 25 years. The report highlights a significant drop in peace operation deployments and funding shortfalls.

World.headline: China Coal Mine Blast Leaves at Least 90 Dead
World.detail: A gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Shanxi province, China, has resulted in the death of at least 90 people, with 38 miners still trapped underground. Chinese leader Xi Jinping has called for an all-out rescue effort and a thorough investigation into the cause of the explosion.

Technology.detail: At the 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Circuits and Systems (ISCAS), HUAWEI presented the Tau (τ) Scaling Law, a new principle proposing time scaling as a guiding principle for the evolution of semiconductors and electronic systems, replacing geometric scaling. This law aims to address the physical limits of Moore's Law and drive innovation in transistor density and system performance.
Technology.headline: HUAWEI Presents the Tau (τ) Scaling Law, Enabling Breakthroughs in Transistor Density and System Performance
China launches Shenzhou-23 spaceship, for new in-orbit stay record, cutting-edge space science
Technology.detail: China successfully launched the Shenzhou-23 crewed spaceship on Sunday, which will send three astronauts to its orbiting Tiangong space station for new tests on long-duration stay and frontier scientific experiments. The mission will conduct over 100 new science and application projects, focusing on space life science, materials science, microgravity fluid physics, aerospace medicine, and new space technologies.

Technology.headline: No immediate chip tariffs expected, but protections important, US says
Technology.detail: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer stated that there are no imminent new US tariffs expected on semiconductors, but protecting the sector with duties is important to facilitate reshoring of chip production. Greer emphasized the importance of properly sequencing any tariffs resulting from the Section 232 national security investigation to promote US output.

A cutting-edge Chromium IX Single-Cell Analysis System has been installed at the Sunshine Coast Health Institute, purchased with funds from the Wishlist Spring Carnival. This technology can identify unique cells, aiding in the diagnosis and treatment of diseases like cancer and autoimmune disorders, promising tailored therapies and improved patient outcomes.
Technology.headline: New tech set to transform cancer treatment on Coast

Technology.detail: Meta is expanding its AI-based age enforcement tools across Facebook and Instagram to identify potentially underage users. This move aims to create safer online experiences by preventing access to age-inappropriate content and features.
Technology.headline: Meta Enlists AI to Enforce Age Restrictions
